text.title: Team RFC Process for Devs Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Team RFC Process for Devs application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Motivation, Proposal, Alternatives, Open Questions, Status. ≤5 sections; >5 = design doc.
rule.rationale: Long RFCs aren't read; 5-section template is the sustainable cap.

rule.statement: Comment window ≥5 business days. Reviewers commit to ≥1 substantive comment per RFC they're tagged in.
rule.rationale: SLA + commitment turns RFC from drift to operational; without, RFCs sit unread.

rule.statement: Draft → Review → Accepted | Rejected | Superseded. Every transition recorded with decider + date.
rule.rationale: Without status, readers act on unfinished proposals; explicit status preserves clarity.

rule.statement: Rejected RFCs archived; reason documented. Future proposals can find 'why we said no'.
rule.rationale: Deleted rejections destroy institutional memory; archive preserves it.

rule.statement: PRs implementing non-trivial decisions reference an accepted RFC. No RFC = PR rejected for design phase.
rule.rationale: Without enforcement, RFCs become optional + decay; PR gating enforces.

rule.statement: Skip Team RFC Process for Devs when any Skip-If condition holds. Do NOT produce an artefact; surface the gap to the requester.
rule.rationale: A methodology applied outside its scope produces low-signal artefacts that pollute the artefact store; the decision tree needs a clean terminator.
